---
subtitle: "Cataloguing Tools for Technology Readiness and Roadmapping"
abstract: |
  This chapter's core contribution is an AI-Powered Bibliometrics Catalogue -- a structured, comparative review of existing AI-enhanced bibliometric and tech-mining tools, organized around two functional axes the rest of the book depends on: support for Technology Readiness Level (TRL) assessment, and support for technology roadmapping.
keywords:
  - AI-powered bibliometrics catalogue
  - tool typology
  - TRL support
  - technology roadmapping
  - semantic clustering
---

# AI Analytics & NLP Extraction {#sec-ai-nlp-extraction}

Bibliometric software has increasingly added AI-assisted modules for extraction, clustering, and summarization. Rather than treat any single package as a fixed pipeline, this chapter catalogues the field: existing tools are compared against two functional axes -- whether they surface Technology Readiness Level (TRL) signals, and whether they support technology roadmapping -- so the comparison stays useful as individual tools change.

A tool only earns an "AI-powered" label in the Catalogue if that claim is substantiated by its own documentation, not inferred from marketing copy.

## An AI-Powered Bibliometrics Catalogue: Scope and Method
- Definition: a catalogue of *existing* AI-enhanced bibliometric and tech-mining tools -- not a single proprietary pipeline -- assembled and scored against this book's two functional axes below.
- **Axis 1, TRL-support:** does the tool surface maturity signals (patent-to-publication ratios, claim-language maturity, funding-stage mentions) usable in the TRL rubric from @sec-scientometric-horizon?
- **Axis 2, roadmapping-support:** does the tool detect technology trajectories, weak signals, and thematic evolution over time?
- Inclusion criteria: documented methodology, reproducibility, active maintenance. A tool is only described as "AI-powered" in the Catalogue if that is substantiated by its own documentation -- not inferred.

::: {.callout-important title="Populate before publication"}
The table below is a scaffold. Each row needs a verified, current citation before this chapter goes to review -- run a fresh literature/tool search rather than relying on memory, since this landscape moves quickly (e.g. established packages regularly add new AI-assisted modules).
:::

| Tool | Data sources | TRL-support signal | Roadmapping signal | Citation |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| *TODO* | | | | |
| *TODO* | | | | |
| *TODO* | | | | |

## Automated Topic Modeling & Semantic Clustering
- Method choice across catalogued tools (e.g., embedding-based clustering vs. classic LDA) and why it matters for reproducibility.

## Citation Networks, Co-Patenting, and Weak-Signal Detection
- How catalogued tools distinguish a genuine weak signal from noise in a sparse corpus.

## From Retrospective Mapping to Prospective Foresight
- Why bibliometrics alone under-determines "what to build next."
- Sets up the design layer introduced in @sec-itu-ecoinnovation-canvas.

::: {.callout-tip title="Bridge to Chapter 3"}
Chapter 3 takes these trajectories and formally aligns them to SDG targets and indicators.
:::
